text.title: Agentic Workflow for Spatial UI
text.summary: What Claude subagents can and cannot do for spatial UI design, with recommended subagents and prompt references.

section.title: Agent Scope
section.p: A Claude subagent can generate spatial UI specifications (layout grids, depth layers, gaze target sizes) from a natural language brief and a target platform. For context-adaptive behaviors, the agent produces a decision matrix mapping detected scene types to UI states. Human review is mandatory before hardware prototyping because agents cannot run XR simulators or validate spatial ergonomics.

rule.statement: All spatial ergonomic decisions require human sign-off before build. Agents cannot simulate XR hardware.
rule.rationale: No text-based agent can validate gaze comfort, motion sickness triggers, or physical ergonomics — these require hardware prototyping with real users.

rule.statement: Personalization model designs produced by agents need human review for privacy compliance (GDPR/CCPA) before implementation.
rule.rationale: Spatial personalization collects behavioral and positional data; privacy impact assessment is required and cannot be delegated to an agent.

list.item: haiku — generating boilerplate spatial layout specs, depth-layer tables, gaze-target checklists
sonnet — scene-type decision matrices, AI capability mapping, interaction pattern documentation

section.title: Prompt and Template References
section.reference: Scene-type to UI state mapping generator
Prompt for generating spatial UI layout and interaction spec
